title: Amarella wislizeni
taxonomy: Amarella wislizeni is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Rosaceae. This plant is native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. It is a perennial herb that grows in the wild and has been used in traditional medicine by the indigenous peoples of the region. The classification of Amarella wislizeni is as follows: kingdom Plantae, clade Angiosperms, clade Eudicots, clade Rosids, order Rosales, family Rosaceae, genus Amarella, species A. wislizeni.
uses: Amarella wislizeni, also known as the White Yarrow or Western Yarrow, has been used in traditional medicine by various Native American tribes. Its leaves and stems contain volatile oils and other compounds that have been reported to have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties.
